name: Desert of Calanda Trail (PR-TE 19)
name_original: Convento del Desierto de Calanda desde Torevelilla (PR-TE 19)
description: Hike the difficult 11.1-mile PR-TE 19 route from Torevelilla to the historic Desert Convent of Calanda, gaining 1234 feet.
region_intro: Hiking around Torrevelilla offers access to varied terrain, characterized by arid landscapes, ravines, and rolling hills. The region features trails that traverse open desert-like areas and follow natural watercourses. Hikers can expect routes that showcase the unique geological formations and sparse vegetation typical of this part of Aragon. Best hiking trails around Torrevelilla The most popular hiking route is Calanda Desert Trail, a 7.5 miles (12.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 31 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the unique desert-like environment. Another top favourite among local hikers is Barranco Fondo del Mezquín Trail, a difficult 12.1 miles (19.4 km) path. This trail navigates through a significant ravine, offering challenging terrain and distinct scenery. Local hikers also love the Calanda loop from Calanda, a 4.4 miles (7.0 km) trail leading through local countryside, often completed in about 1 hour 52 minutes. Hiking around Torrevelilla is defined by desert landscapes, barrancos (ravines), and hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging long-distance trails. The routes in Torrevelilla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a small number of reviews. More than a dozen hikers have used komoot to explore Torrevelilla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Torrevelilla?
A: Hiking around Torrevelilla features varied terrain, primarily characterized by arid landscapes, ravines (barrancos), and rolling hills. Trails often traverse open desert-like areas and follow natural watercourses, showcasing unique geological formations and sparse vegetation typical of this part of Aragon.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Torrevelilla?
A: Yes, Torrevelilla offers at least one easy trail. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31483752" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calanda loop from Calanda&lt;/a&gt; is a 4.4 miles (7.0 km) route that leads through local countryside and can typically be completed in under two hours, making it a good option for beginners or a relaxed walk.

Q: Are there any loop trails available in the Torrevelilla area?
A: Yes, several loop trails are available. Besides the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31483752" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calanda loop from Calanda&lt;/a&gt;, you can also explore the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38151515" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de las Taleras – La Tarayola loop from La Ginebrosa / la Ginebrosa&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 9.1 miles (14.6 km) and offers a more extended experience.

Q: What are the best seasons for hiking in Torrevelilla?
A: Given the arid and desert-like characteristics of the region, spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for hiking in Torrevelilla. Temperatures are milder, making longer excursions more comfortable. Summer can be very hot, while winter can bring cooler temperatures, though snow is less common in the lower elevations.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Torrevelilla?
A: The trails in Torrevelilla are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Hikers often praise the unique desert landscapes, the challenging terrain of the ravines, and the distinct scenery that sets this region apart.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or attractions to see near the hiking routes?
A: Yes, there are several interesting places to explore. You might consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2752464" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Equinox Tunnel&lt;/a&gt;, the historic town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5285409" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calanda&lt;/a&gt;, or the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5290137" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desert Convent of Calanda&lt;/a&gt;. These attractions offer a glimpse into the region's history and unique character.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Torrevelilla suitable for families with children?
A: For families, the easier and shorter loop trails are generally the most suitable. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31483752" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calanda loop from Calanda&lt;/a&gt; is a good option due to its manageable distance and duration. Always check the specific trail difficulty and length to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Torrevelilla?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on trails can vary, generally, dogs are welcome on many hiking trails in rural Aragon, especially if kept on a leash. Given the arid environment, always ensure you carry enough water for both yourself and your dog, as natural water sources may be scarce.

Q: What are some more challenging hiking options in Torrevelilla?
A: For those seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981140695/barranco-fondo-del-mezquin-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco Fondo del Mezquín Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 12.1 miles (19.4 km) path that navigates through a significant ravine, offering demanding terrain. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981380471/desert-of-calanda-trail-pr-te-19" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desert of Calanda Trail (PR-TE 19)&lt;/a&gt;, which is 11.1 miles (17.8 km) long.

Q: Are there any trails that explore the unique desert environment of Torrevelilla?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979730081/calanda-desert-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calanda Desert Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.5 miles (12.0 km) route specifically designed to explore the unique desert-like environment of the region, offering a distinct hiking experience.

Q: Is there public transport access to the trailheads around Torrevelilla?
A: Public transport options directly to specific trailheads in this rural area may be limited. It is advisable to check local bus schedules for connections to nearby towns like Calanda or La Ginebrosa, from which some trails might be accessible by foot or a short taxi ride. Driving to the trailheads is often the most convenient option.

Q: Where can I find parking near the hiking trails in Torrevelilla?
A: Parking is typically available in or near the starting points of trails, especially in the villages or towns from which they depart, such as Calanda or La Ginebrosa. Look for designated parking areas or suitable roadside spots, being mindful of local regulations and private property.
tips: The old Baroque-style Convent is currently in ruins and is a site that attracts many visitors for its particular charm and surrounding environment. Its construction dates from the 18th century and its large size draws a lot of attention. The possible restoration of the same is currently under study, although its high budget means that it is still delayed.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the Convent of the Desert of Calanda from Torevelilla (PR-TE 19) trail?
faqs.answer: The Komoot route for the Convent of the Desert of Calanda from Torevelilla (PR-TE 19) is approximately 17.8 km long and typically takes around 5 hours to complete. Keep in mind that this duration can vary based on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this hiking route?
faqs.answer: This specific route is rated as difficult on Komoot. While some longer versions of the PR-TE 19 are considered intermediate, this particular segment involves significant elevation changes and demanding sections, making it suitable for experienced hikers.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a diverse landscape, traversing through pine forests, unique gypsum formations, and calcareous ridges. You'll also encounter ancient ditches and aqueducts. A notable section is the well-maintained Guadalopillo route, which features walkways leading to the Guadalope River.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The most significant landmark on this route is the impressive 17th-century Desert Convent of Calanda. Hikers often make this their primary destination. The trail also offers views of diverse natural features like gypsum formations and the Guadalope River.

faqs.question: Is the Convent of the Desert of Calanda from Torevelilla (PR-TE 19) trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through natural areas, there is no specific information indicating restrictions for dogs. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no information suggesting that permits or entrance fees are required to hike the Convent of the Desert of Calanda from Torevelilla (PR-TE 19) trail. It appears to be freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: Given the diverse terrain and potential for demanding sections, spring and autumn are generally the best times to hike this route. The weather is typically milder, avoiding the extreme heat of summer and potential winter conditions that could make some sections more challenging.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead in Torrevelilla?
faqs.answer: Torrevelilla serves as a potential starting point for this route. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, as a connection point, you should be able to find suitable parking within or near the village to begin your hike. Look for designated public parking or consult local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, Torrevelilla is mentioned as a possible point for continuing the route or connecting to other trails. Specifically, it can link to the PR-TE 14 (El Pilón viewpoint). The route itself is part of the larger Desierto de Calanda trail system.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from Torrevelilla, a small village. While specific cafes or pubs directly on the trail are unlikely, you may find basic amenities, including accommodation and places to eat, within Torrevelilla or nearby towns like Calanda and Alcañiz. It's always a good idea to check local listings before your visit.

faqs.question: What is a strategic tip for hiking this trail?
faqs.answer: A suggested approach for this area is to hike directly to the Desert Convent of Calanda and then consider returning via the Hermitage of San Elías. This allows you to experience key historical sites and varied landscapes within your hike.
